steps.title: Use the center and a point on the circle
steps.content: A circle with center $(-3,7)$ has equation $(x+3)^2+(y-7)^2=r^2$. Use the given point $(-9,15)$ to find $r^2$.

steps.title: Write the standard form with the center
steps.content:
A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation $(x-h)^2 + (y-k)^2 = r^2$. With center $(-3,7)$:
$$(x-(-3))^2 + (y-7)^2 = r^2 \;\Rightarrow\; (x+3)^2 + (y-7)^2 = r^2$$

steps.title: Find r^2 using the given point
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $(x-3)^2 + (y+7)^2 = 10$ - wrong center signs and $r^2 = 10$. Wrong.
  • B) $(x+3)^2 + (y-7)^2 = 10$ - correct center but $r^2 = 10$ instead of 100. Wrong.
  • C) $(x-3)^2 + (y+7)^2 = 100$ - correct $r^2 = 100$ but center signs are flipped, giving center $(3,-7)$. Wrong.
  • D) $(x+3)^2 + (y-7)^2 = 100$ - correct center $(-3,7)$ and $r^2 = 100$. Correct.
That confirms D is correct.
